mood.label: EXTENDED-RISK-ON
mood.summary: Semis/AI infra dominating again with CRDO +12.9% on guidance, NTAP +12.4% post-earnings beat, ARM +2.8%, SNOW +4.0%. Indices steady (SPY +0.39%) but watchlist beneath shows divergence — NVDA -1.9% sell-the-news fade. With 25% historical drift from too-tight stops on momentum names (see wiki/rubric-stop-too-tight.md), discipline today is sizing + ATR-floor stops, NOT chasing the EP-day pops.

breadth.note: Pre-open: sector ETFs mostly green (XLK, XLF, XLY, XLI, XLU all +). XLE flat. SMH led overnight on CRDO/ARM strength.

fearGreed.label: n/a
fearGreed.note: CNN F&G feed not fetched this run; DeepSeek tab carries the polled value if needed.

indicesNarrative: S&P holding +0.4% with most sector ETFs green. The leadership is in semis (SMH/CIBR/IGV strength overnight), with utilities (XLU +0.78%) acting as a hedge. Watch for whether QQQ leads or follows — if QQQ green and IWM red, that's narrow-tech leadership requiring extra caution on small-cap chases.

sectorsThemes.note: Continuation of NVDA-day rally; CRDO +12.9% on guidance is the new EP-FRESH candidate
NTAP +12.4% on earnings beat + raised guidance — score 82/GO
PTON +10.2% EP setup, screener score 83 highest of day

sectorsNarrative: AI infra remains the dominant theme but is getting crowded — CRDO/ARM extended, MRVL +3%, SNOW +4%. The cleaner setups today are the day-1 EPs (NTAP) and the laggard pivots (PTON 21EMA bounce after sideways base). Avoid chasing CRDO/ARM at full extension; wait for first 21EMA pullback.
industryNarrative: Semiconductors specifically (per OpenD: ARM +2.8%, MRVL +3%, ALAB +3%) are the engine. Computer Processing Hardware (DELL, HPQ, HPE) had a Friday spike — watch for pullback continuation today vs full distribution. NTAP's earnings beat is sector-specific (Computer Peripherals); not yet a broad enterprise tech reset.

industryMovers.note: Guidance-driven EP; RVOL 1.2x (lower than peers — watch volume on day 2)
Earnings beat + raised guidance; RVOL 2.6x; screener verdict GO 82/100
EP-FRESH; RVOL 3.0x; screener verdict GO 83/100 (highest of session)

movers.thesis: EP on guidance — but RVOL only 1.2x suggests weaker conviction than NTAP/PTON. Stop should be 1.5x ATR below entry not the prior-day low (see wiki/rubric-stop-too-tight.md).
Cleanest EP of the day: earnings catalyst, RVOL 2.6x, holding upper end of range. ATR-based stop ~$133. Watch for first-day-of-EP pullback into 21EMA for second entry.
EP-FRESH on a recovery laggard. Screener verdict GO 83 (highest of session) but consumer-services context is unusual — verify volume holds in first hour.

watchlist.note: Sell-the-news post-beat; holding $215 21EMA support. If breaks, opens up $208 retest.
Steady leadership; no setup signal today.
Continuation in AI infra; second-leg from base.

traderLens.name: @Qullamaggie
traderLens.view: NTAP is the cleanest EP-FRESH today: catalyst, RVOL >2x, range hold. ATR-floor stop ~$133. PTON tempting but consumer-services context is off-rubric.

traderLens.name: @markminervini
traderLens.view: Wait. Most of today's movers are day-1 EPs, not VCP breakouts. ALAB closest to Stage-2 continuation but pre-pivot.

traderLens.name: @PrimeTrading_
traderLens.view: NVDA pulling back to 21EMA at $215 is a tradeable level IF it holds today's open.

traderLens.name: @SRxTrades
traderLens.view: PTON if it tightens; NTAP for the swing.

traderLens.name: @jfsrev
traderLens.view: PTON RVOL 3.0x best of session. NTAP 2.6x acceptable. CRDO 1.2x below threshold — pass.

traderLens.name: @Clement_Ang17
traderLens.view: NVDA $215 pullback is the cleanest liquid-leader 21EMA test on the screen.

traderLens.name: @TedHZhang
traderLens.view: NTAP has earnings catalyst + guidance raise + sector tailwind — three pillars align. Position size accordingly.

standout.thesis: Earnings beat + raised guidance + sector tailwind = three-pillar align (@TedHZhang). RVOL 2.6x, clean range hold. Day-1 entry risky at $139.36 — preferred is day-2-3 pullback into post-gap shelf. ATR-floor stop ~$133 per wiki/rubric-stop-too-tight.md.

alert: Day-1 EP chasing is the highest-drift mistake in your audit history (15 stop-too-tight whipsaws across 7 months, 25% of all trades). Today's EPs (CRDO, NTAP, PTON) are tempting — but use 1.5×ATR(14) floor stops, not prior-low. See wiki/rubric-stop-too-tight.md.
